sections.paragraph:
σαφῶς (Hom. [σαφέως]+; ins, pap, LXX; TestSol 4:2 D; Philo; Jos., Bell. 2, 104 ς. ἐπίσταμαι, Ant. 4, 41) adv. of σαφής (comp. σαφέστερον Just., A I, 35, 2) pert. to high degree of exactitude, clearly, exactly, very well εἰδέναι (Pla., Ep. 6, 323d, Ep. 7, 324a; Diod S 19, 9, 2; Appian, Bell. Civ. 3, 82 §335; PCairZen 110, 12 [257 b.c.]) 1 Cl 62:3; PtK 3 p. 15, 26. μανθάνειν (Menand., Epitr. 332 S. [156 Kö.]; Mitt-Wilck. I/2, 6, 4) Dg 11:2. (W. ἐπιμελῶς) πυνθάνεσθαι (Menand., Epitr. 877 S. [557 Kö.]) Dg 1.—DELG s.v. σάφα.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

comp. comp. = comparative(ly)

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

PCairZen PCairZen = Zenon Papyri, Catalogue général . . . du Musée du Caire—List 4

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

PtK PtK = Petruskerygma (Preaching of Peter)—List 1

Menand Menand , various dramas cited; IV–III b.c.—List 5

Mitt-Wilck. Mitt-Wilck. = LMitteis/UWilcken, Grundzüge und Chrestomathie der Papyruskunde—Lists 4, 6

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 917
